metadata.title: Azure Foundry LLM in Fusion Agent Studio - Quickstart Guide
metadata.source: Azure Foundry Docs + Agent Framework + Microsoft Fusion

overview: The easiest way to use Azure Foundry LLMs in Fusion Agent Studio is through the Agent Framework SDK, which abstracts away complexity and provides Python/TypeScript SDKs with just a few lines of code.

quickstart_3_steps.title: Create Azure Foundry Project & Deploy Model
steps: Go to https://ai.azure.com (Azure AI Foundry portal)
Sign in with your Azure account
Copy endpoint URL from project overview

quickstart_3_steps.title: Set Up Agent Framework
setup: Get token: az account get-access-token --scope https://ai.azure.com/.default

quickstart_3_steps.title: Write Agent Code (5 lines)
quickstart_3_steps.code:
import asyncio
from agent_framework import Agent
from agent_framework.foundry import FoundryChatClient
from azure.identity import AzureCliCredential

async def main():
    # Initialize agent
    client = FoundryChatClient(
        credential=AzureCliCredential(),
        endpoint=os.environ['FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT']
    )
    
    # Create agent
    agent = Agent(client=client, model=os.environ['FOUNDRY_MODEL'])
    
    # Run agent
    response = await agent.chat("What's 2+2?")
    print(response)

asyncio.run(main())
quickstart_3_steps.notes: That's it. Azure Foundry + Agent Framework = working agent.

method_comparison.description: Three ways to integrate Azure Foundry LLM with agents

full_code_example.title: Complete Working Example
setup: Get token: az account get-access-token --scope https://ai.azure.com/.default
full_code_example.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import asyncio
from agent_framework import Agent
from agent_framework.foundry import FoundryChatClient
from azure.identity import AzureCliCredential

async def main():
    # 1. Create client pointing to your Azure Foundry endpoint
    credential = AzureCliCredential()
    endpoint = os.environ.get('FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT', 'https://your-project.ai.azure.com')
    
    client = FoundryChatClient(
        credential=credential,
        endpoint=endpoint
    )
    
    # 2. Create agent with your deployed model
    model_name = os.environ.get('FOUNDRY_MODEL', 'your-deployment-name')
    agent = Agent(
        client=client,
        model=model_name,
        system_prompt="You are a helpful research assistant."
    )
    
    # 3. Use the agent
    messages = [
        "What is Azure Foundry?",
        "How do I deploy an LLM model?",
        "Compare REST API vs Agent Framework"
    ]
    
    for message in messages:
        print(f"\nUser: {message}")
        response = await agent.chat(message)
        print(f"Agent: {response}")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    asyncio.run(main())
full_code_example.output:
User: What is Azure Foundry?
Agent: Azure Foundry is a fully managed service...

User: How do I deploy an LLM model?
Agent: To deploy a model: 1) Create project in Foundry portal, 2) Select model, 3) Deploy to endpoint...

User: Compare REST API vs Agent Framework
Agent: Agent Framework is easier (SDK handles auth, scaling), REST API gives more control...

common_issues.solution: Run: az login && az account get-access-token --scope https://ai.azure.com/.default
Copy endpoint from Azure Foundry portal > Project Overview > Libraries > Foundry
Wait 2-5 minutes after deployment. Check status in Foundry portal.
Tokens expire in 60-90 minutes. Get fresh: az account get-access-token
Agent Framework handles this automatically. Add backoff: await agent.chat(msg, retry_count=3)

next_steps: 1. Create your first Foundry project (5 min)
4. Add function calling for tools (10 min)
5. Build your custom agent logic (depends on use case)
summary: Azure Foundry + Agent Framework = 3 steps, ~10 minutes, production-ready LLM agents. No complex setup. Focus on your agent logic, let Azure handle the infrastructure.
